hawkish_phrases: Monetary policy will need to continue to remain restrictive for sufficiently long
domestic inflationary pressures are moderating, but they remain somewhat elevated
CPI inflation is expected to rise quite sharply in the near term, to 3.7%
the threshold for second-round effects might now be somewhat lower
constrained supply relative to demand...could sustain domestic price and wage pressures, consistent with a relatively tighter monetary policy path
dovish_phrases: GDP growth has been weaker than expected
indicators of business and consumer confidence have declined
greater or longer-lasting weakness in demand relative to supply...could push down on inflationary pressures, warranting a less restrictive path of Bank Rate
weakening activity and a looser labour market could weigh further on inflation
subdued outlook for demand remained consistent with CPI inflation staying sustainably at the target in the medium term
summary: The MPC voted 7-2 to cut Bank Rate by 25bp to 4.5%, with two members dissenting in favor of a 50bp cut, emphasizing gradual withdrawal of restraint while maintaining a restrictive stance amid moderating but elevated domestic inflationary pressures and weaker-than-expected growth.
